hypothesis: If the Force Delta metric captures genuine physical inconsistency in the learned potential energy surface, then incorporating it as a regularization term during fine-tuning of direct-force pre-trained models—penalizing the magnitude of U∆across training configurations—will simultaneously improve force prediction accuracy on out-of-distribution structures and reduce the non-conservativity of the force field, as measured by the curl of the predicted force, without requiring additional DFT reference data beyond what was used in the original pre-training.
